items.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
items.raw_content: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.
items.summary: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

items.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
items.raw_content: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthropic's Mythos. The cybersecurity model […]
items.summary: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyb

items.title: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
items.raw_content: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters. In an order on Monday, Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín writes that the settlement will provide "meaningful relief," offering authors around $3,000 for each book […]
items.summary: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters.

items.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
items.raw_content: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.
items.summary: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

items.title: America needs to stop getting shocked by Chinese AI
items.raw_content: Last week, two Chinese AI companies unveiled models they say can credibly compete with the best systems from OpenAI and Anthropic. The response was swift and predictable. Markets wobbled, commentators declared Silicon Valley shooketh, and policymakers reached for the familiar language of arms races and wake-up calls. In one headline, The Associated Press said a […]

items.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
items.raw_content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.
items.summary: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

items.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.raw_content: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.
items.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

items.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
items.raw_content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.
items.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

items.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
items.raw_content: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.
items.summary: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

items.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
items.raw_content: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.
items.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

items.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
items.raw_content: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.
items.summary: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

items.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
items.raw_content: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this […]

items.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
items.raw_content: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. items.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
items.raw_content: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.
items.summary: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

items.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
items.raw_content: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.
items.summary: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

items.title: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
items.raw_content: I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see, and my 30-minute demo left me with achy eyeballs. It was an interesting concept with terrible execution, especially compared to the […]

items.title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
items.raw_content: China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming […]

items.title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
items.raw_content: Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated with a suite of generative AI tools. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own Firefly AI models. Adobe describes the […]

items.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
items.raw_content: Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. items.title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
